core_mechanism: Field-mediated mutual coupling of self-driven phase oscillators, in which each unit both generates and responds to a shared long-range field in the surrounding medium, producing emergent phase synchronization together with an effective attraction that self-organizes the units into clusters
brief_reason: The abstract states that each beating unit creates a flow field through which the units interact, yielding two coupling effects—synchronization of beating phases and attraction—that drive swarm/cluster formation with a power-law cluster-size dependence on the spread of intrinsic frequencies.
